questions.question: How is sleep efficiency calculated?

options.text: Total sleep time minus time spent awake
(Total sleep time / time in bed) × 100
Number of sleep cycles completed per night
Deep sleep hours divided by total sleep hours

questions.explanation: Sleep efficiency is calculated as (Total Sleep Time / Time in Bed) × 100. For example, sleeping 6.5 hours out of 8 hours in bed gives you about 81% sleep efficiency.

questions.question: What is Non-Sleep Deep Rest (NSDR)?

options.text: A type of medication for sleep
Lying quietly with eyes closed, which is still restorative even without sleeping
The deepest stage of NREM sleep
A form of vigorous exercise before bed

questions.explanation: Non-Sleep Deep Rest (NSDR) is lying quietly in bed with eyes closed. Research shows this still allows your body to enter parasympathetic mode, reduce cortisol, and support recovery -- even without sleeping.

questions.question: What is the most powerful tool for resetting your circadian clock after jet lag?

options.text: Taking melatonin supplements
Morning sunlight at your destination
Sleeping as much as possible on arrival
Exercising vigorously before bed

questions.explanation: Morning sunlight at your destination is the most powerful tool for rapid adjustment after travel. Light is the strongest signal to reset your circadian clock.

questions.question: What should you protect above all else during high-stress periods?

options.text: Your exercise routine
Your wind-down ritual
Your caffeine intake
Your social calendar

questions.explanation: During high-stress periods, protecting your wind-down ritual is the most important strategy. It creates a buffer between stress and rest and becomes even more important when your mind is racing.

questions.question: What are the four categories of your personalized sleep toolkit?

options.text: Morning, afternoon, evening, and nighttime tools
Foundational, mental, environmental, and wellness tools
Physical, emotional, social, and spiritual tools
Prevention, intervention, recovery, and maintenance tools

questions.explanation: Your personalized sleep toolkit spans four categories: foundational tools (sleep diary, sleep window), mental tools (3 C's, Cognitive Shuffle), environmental tools (stimulus control, sleep sanctuary), and wellness tools (nutrition, NSDR).

title: Your Resilient and Adaptable Sleep System - Quiz
